- Top-right: purple (most of it)
- Bottom-left: white (small triangle)
So the white stamp from above DID change the bottom block. It covered most of the block with white, leaving only the bottom-right 5x5 square as purple.
Let me think about this more carefully. The stamping mechanic seems to be:
1. The stamp covers a portion of the bottom block based on the block's position and shape.
2. The stamp overwrites the existing color in the covered portion with the block's color.
When I stamp white from above:
- The stamp covers the top portion of the bottom block.
- The stamp overwrites the existing color in the top portion with white.
When I stamp purple from below:
- The stamp covers the bottom portion of the bottom block.
- The stamp overwrites the existing color in the bottom portion with purple.
The issue is that the stamps don't cover the entire top/bottom portions. They cover specific regions based on the block's shape.
